title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people who share their passion for cycling or other outdoor pursuits. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: Out There: Four People on Cycling and Outdoor Activities

texts.A:
### Petra, 38, Physiotherapist
As a physiotherapist, I see the consequences of sedentary lifestyles every day, which was part of what motivated me to start cycling seriously four years ago. [43]I entered a sportive – a long-distance non-competitive cycling event – on something of a whim, trained for eight weeks on a borrowed bike, and came home with a passion I genuinely hadn't anticipated.[/43] Now I cycle nearly every weekend and have completed events in France, Spain and the Netherlands. [47]The hardest part is the time commitment, which occasionally creates friction with my partner – though we've worked out a compromise where I do most of my long rides on Sunday mornings when he's playing football.[/47] [51]Last year I set up a women's cycling group in my town, and watching women who thought they couldn't ride long distances discover that they absolutely can is genuinely deeply satisfying.[/51]
texts.B:
### Alistair, 53, Solicitor
[44]I started hiking seriously during lockdown, when my firm had moved entirely online and I realised I was barely leaving the house from one day to the next.[/44] I drove to the Peak District one weekend with a borrowed map, walked for two days and slept under the stars in a bivvy bag, and something about that experience remade something in me. [48]What I value most about being in wild country is the complete absence of professional obligation – there are no clients, no emails, no deadlines when you're navigating moorland in the rain.[/48] [52]I've become almost evangelical about encouraging colleagues to try it – I must have loaned out my spare walking boots to half the firm at this point.[/52]
texts.C:
### Yemi, 30, Nurse
[45]I started kayaking after a colleague brought me along to a taster session at a local watersports centre, and being on the water immediately reminded me of growing up near the coast in Nigeria.[/45] I had no technical skills at all, but the instructors were patient and I progressed quickly. [49]What I value most about kayaking is the way it demands your complete attention – when you're navigating white water, you are not thinking about anything else, and that absolute present-tense focus is the best stress relief I've found.[/49] I now paddle at least twice a week and I'm working towards my British Canoeing two-star qualification.
texts.D:
### Brenda, 66, Retired Teacher
[46]I started Nordic walking after my GP recommended a low-impact form of exercise following a knee replacement three years ago, and within six months I was completely hooked.[/46] The poles engage the upper body in a way that makes the workout considerably more effective than ordinary walking, without the strain on the joints. [50]More than the physical benefits, what has kept me going is the group I walk with every Thursday morning – a dozen retired women who are, without exaggeration, the funniest and most interesting people I know.[/50] We've extended our range to include overnight walking weekends, which I would never have thought myself capable of at sixty-six.

questions.question: became enthusiastic about their activity after participating in an event without much prior planning?
questions.explanation: Petra entered a long-distance cycling event on a whim, trained briefly on a borrowed bike, and came home with an unexpected passion.

questions.question: took up their outdoor activity in response to restrictions on movement that had affected their daily life?
questions.explanation: Alistair started hiking seriously during lockdown when his firm moved online and he realised he was barely leaving the house.

questions.question: was introduced to their activity by a colleague and found it connected to memories of earlier life?
questions.explanation: Yemi started kayaking after a colleague took her to a taster session, and being on water reminded her of growing up near the coast in Nigeria.

questions.question: began their outdoor activity on medical advice following a health problem?
questions.explanation: Brenda started Nordic walking after her GP recommended low-impact exercise following a knee replacement.

questions.question: describes a recurring source of tension in their personal relationships caused by the time their activity requires?
questions.explanation: Petra says the time commitment occasionally creates friction with her partner, though they have worked out a compromise.

questions.question: values their outdoor activity primarily because it removes them completely from professional pressures?
questions.explanation: Alistair says what he values most is the complete absence of professional obligation – no clients, emails or deadlines in wild country.

questions.question: describes their activity as requiring total concentration and providing effective relief from stress?
questions.explanation: Yemi says kayaking demands complete attention and that the resulting present-tense focus is the best stress relief she has found.

questions.question: mentions that the social aspect of their activity has become as important to them as the physical one?
questions.explanation: Brenda says what has kept her going, more than the physical benefits, is the group she walks with every Thursday morning.

questions.question: describes setting up a group to help others take part in an activity they were not sure they were capable of?
questions.explanation: Petra set up a women's cycling group and describes watching women who doubted themselves discover they can ride long distances.

questions.question: describes enthusiastically promoting their outdoor pursuit to colleagues in their professional environment?
questions.explanation: Alistair says he has become almost evangelical about encouraging colleagues to try hiking and has loaned out his spare walking boots to half the firm.
